findings.field: Professional Summary; TikTok Experience bullets 1, 2, 4; DiDi Experience bullet 1
findings.issue: Modifier/ownership adjectives are bolded repeatedly (`team-maintained`, `team-owned`, `bounded`). The rubric treats bold emphasis as reserved for technologies and scale/scope signals, so this adds visual noise.
findings.fix: Remove bold from qualifiers and ownership adjectives. Keep bold only on technologies, systems, and quantified scope/results.

findings.issue: The opening framing leads with a transition narrative (`Transitioning from data analytics to software engineering`) instead of a direct SDET/backend signal, which weakens the first-screen read.
findings.fix: Rewrite the first sentence to open with the target role and domain, e.g. `SDET / backend engineer:` followed by test infrastructure, REST/gRPC, and distributed-systems validation.

findings.issue: The category title is too vague for quick scanning and mixes testing/CI tools with source-control and framework entries.
findings.fix: Rename the category to something information-rich such as `Testing & CI` or split it into `Testing` and `CI/CD`.

findings.field: JD minimum qualifications vs. Professional Summary and TikTok Experience / project bullets
findings.issue: The resume shows broad API/test automation experience, but it does not explicitly evidence the JD's harder SDET expectations: from-scratch test framework design, webhook/idempotency/retry edge cases, and incident post-mortem prevention steps.
findings.fix: Add one bullet in the most relevant testing/backend experience that states you designed the framework/validation pack from scratch, included seeded/cleaned test data, and covered REST/webhook idempotency, retry, and out-of-order cases; if applicable, add a post-mortem-derived prevention step.

revision_priority: [MUST] Rewrite the first Summary sentence to lead with `SDET/backend engineer` signal, not the transition narrative.

[MUST] Add one concrete bullet showing from-scratch test framework ownership plus API edge-case coverage (idempotency, retry, webhook, out-of-order delivery) and, if true, a post-mortem prevention action.
[SHOULD] Rename the `APIs` skills category to `Testing & CI` or split it into clearer buckets.
[NICE] Remove bold emphasis from qualifiers like `team-maintained` and `team-owned` so emphasis stays on technical and quantitative signals.
raw_json.revision_instructions: Rewrite Summary sentence 1 to open as `SDET/backend engineer` with test-infra, REST/gRPC, distributed-systems, and CI/CD language; remove the transition-first framing. In the TikTok section, add one bullet that explicitly says you designed a reusable test framework/validation pack from scratch, seeded/cleaned SQL test data, and covered REST/webhook idempotency, retry, and out-of-order delivery cases; include a post-mortem prevention step only if you actually owned one. Rename the `APIs` Skills category to `Testing & CI` or split it into clearer buckets. Remove bolding from modifier words like `team-maintained` and `team-owned`; keep bold for technologies, scope, and metrics.

[SHOULD] Rename the `APIs` skills category to `Testing & CI` or split it into clearer buckets.
[NICE] Remove bold emphasis from qualifiers like `team-maintained` and `team-owned` so emphasis stays on technical and quantitative signals.
